items.q: Does being fit mean the same as being healthy?
items.correct: No - you can be fit but unhealthy (e.g. injured or ill), and healthy without being very fit.
items.misconception: Fitness and health are exactly the same thing.
items.random_wrong: Health only means having strong muscles.
items.why: Health is overall wellbeing; fitness is the ability to meet physical demands, so the two can differ.

items.q: What does a sedentary lifestyle lead to?
items.correct: An increased risk of obesity, heart disease and type 2 diabetes.
items.random_wrong: No effect on health at all.
items.why: Lack of activity lowers fitness and energy expenditure, raising the risk of several health problems.

items.q: What does a balanced diet require?
items.correct: The right amount of energy and the correct proportions of all nutrient groups.
items.random_wrong: Eating only protein to stay healthy.
items.why: All nutrient groups, including some fat, are needed; balance means proportion, not elimination.

items.q: The World Health Organisation defines health as:
items.correct: Complete physical, mental and social wellbeing
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Complete physical, mental and social wellbeing. “The absence of illness only” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
It's Heart, lungs and blood vessels. “Muscles and joints” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: A sedentary lifestyle increases the risk of:
items.why: It's Obesity and heart disease. “Improved stamina” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Which BMI value is generally classed as obese?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Over 30. “Below 18.5” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: A person has a mass of 90 kg and a height of 2.0 m. What is their BMI?
items.why: The real figure is 22.5 kg/m^2. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
